briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines rather than genuine artificial intelligence.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware firm Synapse, highlighting serious risks in the Banking-as-a-Service model.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products into increasingly niche and unconventional territory, reshaping how consumers think about credit.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deepening ambitions in financial services and credit decisioning.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada officially flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, a milestone relevant to the country's still-evolving consumer-driven banking framework.

- **[CVCA and NACO offer competing visions for feds' $750-million venture envelope](https://betakit.com/cvca-and-naco-offer-competing-visions-for-feds-750-million-venture-envelope/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's two leading venture capital associations are presenting rival proposals for how the federal government should deploy its $750-million venture funding commitment, with implications for fintech startups nationwide.

- **[How Gaiia used its R&D to help finance a pivot from services to software](https://betakit.com/how-gaiia-used-its-rd-to-help-finance-a-pivot-from-services-to-software/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian startup Gaiia leveraged R&D tax credits and financing strategies to successfully transition its business model, offering a playbook relevant to early-stage Atlantic Canadian tech and fintech companies.

- **[UniUni raises $85 million USD as it scales gig-powered delivery across North America](https://betakit.com/uniuni-raises-85-million-usd-as-it-scales-gig-powered-delivery-across-north-america/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian last-mile delivery startup UniUni has raised $85 million USD to expand its gig-economy-powered logistics network, reflecting strong investor appetite for Canadian tech at scale.

- **[Canadians feel the pinch at the pump as conflict in the Middle East drives up gas prices](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/gas-prices-soar-canada-middle-east-conflict-9.7118590?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Surging oil prices tied to Middle East conflict are hitting Canadian consumers hard at the pump, with broader implications for household finances and cost-of-living pressures relevant to financial inclusion discussions.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This community resource catalogues the growing risks and failures within fintech, offering a sobering counterpoint to innovation narratives and useful context for regulators and ecosystem builders in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Agentic AI Startup Lyzr Raises Funds at $250 Million Valuation](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/agentic-ai-startup-lyzr-raises-funds-at-250-million-valuation)** — *Financial Post* — Lyzr AI, backed by Accenture, has reached a $250M valuation building enterprise AI agent infrastructure — a signal of where embedded AI in financial services is heading and a benchmark for Atlantic Canadian startups watching the VC landscape.

- **[CVCA and NACO offer competing visions for feds' $750-million venture envelope](https://betakit.com/cvca-and-naco-offer-competing-visions-for-feds-750-million-venture-envelope/)** — *BetaKit* — The debate over Canada's $750-million venture capital envelope has direct implications for Atlantic Canadian fintech founders seeking federal funding support, making the outcome of these competing proposals critical to watch.
